task: I’ve got a doctor follow-up and a couple of appointment reminders to sort out, and I want to make sure I’m not missing anything between the visit, the paperwork, and what happens after. What’s the simplest way to keep it all straight without relying on memory?

turns.agent_response:
The simplest way is to use one small **follow-up tracker** for each appointment and keep everything in the same order every time:

### 6-line note format
| Item | What to write |
|---|---|
| 1. Appointment | Doctor / date / time |
| 2. Reason | Follow-up, reminder, test, etc. |
| 3. Paperwork | Forms, letters, referrals, receipts |
| 4. Numbers | **Case # / account # / reference #** |
| 5. Next step | Call, schedule, lab work, pickup, etc. |
| 6. Deadline | By when it needs to be done |

### Simple routine
- **Before the visit:** write the appointment and any questions.
- **At the visit:** jot down any instructions, paperwork, and numbers.
- **After the visit:** write the next step and deadline right away.
- **Keep all reminders in one place:** phone notes, paper notebook, or one page per appointment.

### Easy rule
If it might matter later, write it down immediately:
- who you saw
- what they said
- any **account/case numbers**
- what you need to do next

If you want, I can turn this into a **fillable template** you can copy for each appointment.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s the right idea, but I’d want it a little tighter. One page per appointment, same order every time, and room for the junk they always spring on you.

I’d use:
- date/time
- doctor / office name
- why I’m there
- what they told me
- any forms or papers they handed me
- case number / reference number / phone number
- what I still have to do
- by when

And I’d keep the actual papers clipped to that page, not scattered around. After the visit, I’d write the next step before I leave the parking lot. Once I get home, I’m not trusting memory. That’s how things get lost.

If you want, make it into a plain fill-in form I can print and use.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a plain one-page fill-in form you can print and use for each appointment.

## Appointment Follow-Up Page

**Date/Time:** _______________________________

**Doctor / Office Name:** _______________________________

**Why I’m there:** _______________________________

**What they told me:**  
__________________________________________________  
__________________________________________________  

**Forms / papers handed to me:**  
__________________________________________________  
__________________________________________________  

**Case / reference / phone number:**  
__________________________________________________  

**What I still have to do:**  
__________________________________________________  

**By when:** _______________________________

**Notes:**  
__________________________________________________  
__________________________________________________  

## Simple rule
- Put **all papers for that visit clipped to this page**
- Fill in the **next step before you leave**
- Don’t rely on memory once you get home

If you want, I can also make this into a **shorter 6-line version** or a **clean table version** for printing.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah. That works. Simple, one page, same order. No fluff.

That’s the kind of thing I’d actually use: the blanks are there, the papers stay clipped together, and it forces the next step down before I walk out. Good enough.
